Triggers: pruna, p-image, pruna image, fast image generation, optimized flux, pruna ai, p image, fast ai image, economic image generation, cheap image generation"

security_audit.summary: This skill has low-risk features: network. No dangerous patterns detected.

content.user_title: Generate optimized images with Pruna P-Image models
content.value_statement: This skill enables rapid, high-quality image generation and editing using Pruna's optimized models via the inference.sh CLI. It addresses the need for fast and economical AI visual content creation by providing access to optimized versions of popular models like FLUX and P-Image. Users can perform text-to-image generation, apply specific artistic styles via LoRA presets, edit existing images, and composite multiple images, all while benefiting from Pruna's speed optimizations that maintain quality without the high computational cost typically associated with diffusion models.
actual_capabilities: Application of pre-defined LoRA style presets
Access to optimized FLUX and other model variants
limitations: Requires installation and configuration of the inference.sh CLI
Dependent on external Pruna/inference.sh service availability
LoRA styles are limited to the provided presets (custom LoRA loading not explicitly detailed in basic usage)

use_cases.title: Rapid Prototyping and App Integration
use_cases.description: Integrate fast image generation into applications or scripts using the CLI. Ideal for generating thumbnails, mockups, or assets where speed and cost are critical factors.

use_cases.title: Stylized Art Creation
use_cases.description: Generate images with specific artistic aesthetics using built-in LoRA presets like 'cinematic-movie-style' or 'neon-punk' without needing to manually tune model weights.

use_cases.title: Product Visualization
use_cases.description: Create professional product photos or marketing visuals by using text prompts to generate studio-quality images or edit existing product shots.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating a square image of a specific subject
prompt_templates.prompt: infsh app run pruna/p-image --input '{"prompt": "professional product photo of sneakers, studio lighting", "aspect_ratio": "1:1"}'

prompt_templates.title: Stylized Generation with LoRA
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating an image with a specific artistic style
prompt_templates.prompt: infsh app run pruna/p-image-lora --input '{"prompt": "portrait of a woman in golden hour light", "lora_preset": "photos-realism"}'

prompt_templates.title: Image Editing
prompt_templates.scenario: Modifying the background of an existing image
prompt_templates.prompt: infsh app run pruna/p-image-edit --input '{"prompt": "change the background to a beach", "images": ["https://your-image.jpg"]}'

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Image Compositing
prompt_templates.scenario: Combining multiple images into a single collage
prompt_templates.prompt: infsh app run pruna/p-image-edit --input '{"prompt": "combine these images into a collage", "images": ["https://img1.jpg", "https://img2.jpg"]}'

output_examples.input: infsh app run pruna/p-image --input '{"prompt": "a futuristic cityscape at sunset"}'
output: Processing prompt: 'a futuristic cityscape at sunset'

best_practices: Use specific aspect ratios (e.g., 16:9 for landscapes, 9:16 for portraits) to match your intended output format.
Leverage the 'pruna/flux-klein-4b' model for extremely fast and economic generation when high fidelity is not the primary requirement.
Utilize the available LoRA presets in P-Image-LoRA to achieve consistent styles without extra prompt engineering.
Ensure you are logged in via `infsh login` before running generation commands to avoid authentication errors.
anti_patterns: Using the base P-Image model when a specific style is needed; switch to P-Image-LoRA for better stylistic control.
Requesting extremely high resolutions or complex edits via the basic model when a specialized editing model (P-Image-Edit) is more appropriate.
Forgetting to escape quotes within the JSON input string when running commands from a terminal.

faq.question: How do I get started with the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: You can install the CLI and necessary tools by running: `npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools`.

faq.question: What LoRA presets are available?
faq.answer: Available presets include: photos-realism, pixel-art, japanese-modern-look, cinematic-movie-style, graffiti-splash, neon-punk, anime-2-5d, ethereal-portrait, retro-90s-style, ink-sketchbook, and paper-cut.

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: The skill itself is a tool to access the Pruna models via inference.sh. Costs depend on the specific model used (e.g., FLUX Klein 4B is noted as very cheap at $0.0001/image), but you generally need an account with the inference.sh platform.
